title: cestrum amictum var. longifolium
intro: Cestrum amictum var. longifolium, also known as the long-leaf cestrum, is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The long-leaf cestrum is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the long-leaf cestrum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
